content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are trying to watch a cricket game. They have already decided a row of NNN seats where they will seat, but they are yet to figure out the exact 222 seats they will buy.

The iii-th seat in the row costs AiA_iAi​ coins to buy. Obviously, they have to sit next to each other, so they will only buy adjacent seats.

Can you figure out the minimum cost for them to buy 222 seats such that they are able to sit together?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of seats in the row.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of buying 222 adjacent seats.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are only 222 seats in the row, so they have to buy those 222. They cost a total of 5+105 + 105+10 coins.
Test Case 2 : They can choose to buy seat numbered 111 and seat numbered 222 for a cost of 1+3=41 + 3 = 41+3=4.
